title: Chromolaena costatipes
intro: Chromolaena costatipes, also known as 'Costa's ironweed', is a plant species in the aster family that is native to the Americas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Chromolaena costatipes include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas and infusions, as well as extracts and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Chromolaena costatipes belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a species of flowering plant within the genus Chromolaena. Chromolaena costatipes is a member of the tribe Eupatorieae. This species is native to tropical regions of the Americas. Chromolaena costatipes is also known by other names, such as Jacobinia costatipes.
